fields.Text:
Fatigue, bone pain, hypoalbuminemia, and renal insufficiency with this bone scan pattern. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Metastatic calcifications}}
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Soft tissue uptake in the thyroid, lungs, stomach, liver, and kidneys supports metastatic calcification.

fields.Text:
Three-hour delayed Tc-99m MDP images. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Severe rhabdomyolysis}}
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Severe rhabdomyolysis can produce prominent soft tissue/muscle uptake on delayed Tc-99m MDP imaging.

fields.Text:
21-year-old athlete with bilateral linear tibial uptake. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Shin splints}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Shin splints show irregular linear uptake along the posteromedial and anterolateral tibiae.

fields.Text:
21-year-old athlete with focal tibial uptake. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Tibial stress fracture}}
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Tibial stress fracture is more focal than the elongated linear uptake of shin splints.

fields.Text:
21-year-old athlete with this tibial bone scan pattern. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Shin splints}}
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Three-phase bone scintigraphy can help distinguish shin splints from tibial stress fracture.

fields.Text:
21-year-old athlete with this tibial bone scan finding. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Tibial stress fracture}}
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. A focal cortical tibial abnormality is the key visual distinction from shin splints.

fields.Text:
36-year-old woman with diffuse bone pain and asymmetric breast uptake on bone scintigraphy. Most concerning diagnosis?

{{c1::Breast malignancy}}
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Asymmetric right breast uptake is concerning for malignancy; diagnostic mammography is the next step.

fields.Text:
2-year-old with this bone scintigraphy pattern. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Neuroblastoma}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Neuroblastoma demonstrates bone-scan avidity in about 30-50% of cases because of microcalcification.

fields.Text:
22-year-old woman with persistent right lower extremity pain after a car accident. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Complex regional pain syndrome}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. CRPS can show abnormal regional periarticular uptake on three-phase bone scintigraphy.

fields.Text:
Bone scan with renal cortical uptake, atrophic spleen, and heterogeneous long bone activity. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Sickle cell anemia}}
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. The pattern includes renal cortical uptake, splenic atrophy, and long-bone heterogeneity from prior infarcts.

fields.Text:
Metastatic prostate cancer with diffusely increased soft tissue radiotracer retention below the thorax. Vascular abnormality?

{{c1::IVC obstruction}}
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. The Wader sign is diffuse soft tissue retention below the thorax with normal clearance above that level.

fields.Text:
Mandibular bone scan finding shown. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Osteonecrosis of the mandible}}
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. The same study also shows left hydronephrosis, likely due to obstruction.

fields.Text:
After partial glossectomy, segmental mandibulectomy, and left radial forearm free flap. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Nonviable flap}}
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Absent radiotracer uptake in the mandible, best seen on SPECT, supports a nonviable flap.

fields.Text:
Skull findings on bone scintigraphy in a patient with prostate cancer. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Hyperostosis frontalis}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Hyperostosis frontalis can mimic skull metastases but has a characteristic frontal calvarial distribution.

fields.Text:
What cervical lymph node level is represented by the light blue and white regions?

{{c1::Level X}}
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. For routine radiology, describe very high mastoid/occipital nodes anatomically as retroauricular/retromastoid or suboccipital nodes unless using the expanded radiation oncology atlas.

fields.Text:
What cervical lymph node level is represented by the dark blue region?

{{c1::Level VIII}}
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Level VIII refers to parotid-region nodes in the expanded nodal atlas.

fields.Text:
What cervical lymph node level is represented by the red and pinkish-purple regions?

{{c1::Level VII}}
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Level VII includes retropharyngeal/retro-styloid nodal regions in the expanded nodal atlas.

fields.Text:
What cervical lymph node level is represented by the light blue and hot pink regions?

{{c1::Level I}}
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Level I includes submental IA and submandibular IB nodes.

fields.Text:
What cervical lymph node level is represented by the yellow and green regions?

{{c1::Level II}}
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Level II spans skull base to lower hyoid, posterior to the submandibular gland and anterior to posterior SCM.

fields.Text: A normal DEXA T-score is {{c1::-1.0 or higher}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Parallel T-score thresholds: normal, osteopenia, osteoporosis.

fields.Text: Osteopenia/low bone mass is a DEXA T-score {{c1::between -1.0 and -2.5}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Parallel T-score thresholds: normal, osteopenia, osteoporosis.

fields.Text: Osteoporosis is a DEXA T-score {{c1::-2.5 or lower}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Parallel T-score thresholds: normal, osteopenia, osteoporosis.

fields.Text: {{c1::Z-scores}} are used for bone density evaluation in {{c2::premenopausal women, men younger than 50 years, and children younger than 20 years}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. In practical terms, use Z-scores for essentially all patients under 50.

fields.Text: Lung metastases from {{c1::Ewing sarcoma}} are {{c2::not expected}} to show intense Tc-99m MDP uptake.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. This contrasts with osteosarcoma lung metastases.

fields.Text: Lung metastases from {{c1::osteosarcoma}} can show {{c2::intense Tc-99m MDP uptake}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Osteoid-producing metastases can be bone-scan avid.

fields.Text: Shin splints show irregular linear uptake along the {{c1::posteromedial and anterolateral tibiae}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. This is the key pattern distinction from focal tibial stress fracture.

fields.Text: {{c1::Three-phase bone scintigraphy}} can help diagnose and distinguish {{c2::shin splints}} from {{c3::tibial stress fractures}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. This is the practical reason to know the scan protocol.

fields.Text: Neuroblastomas are avid on bone scintigraphy about {{c1::30-50%}} of the time because they can contain {{c2::microcalcification}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Rhabdomyosarcoma and Wilms tumor do not typically show this avidity.

fields.Text: {{c1::Rhabdomyosarcoma}} is {{c2::not expected}} to demonstrate avidity on bone scintigraphy.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. This contrasts with neuroblastoma.

fields.Text: {{c1::Wilms tumor}} is {{c2::not expected}} to demonstrate avidity on bone scintigraphy.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. This contrasts with neuroblastoma.

fields.Text: {{c1::Samarium-153 EDTMP}} is an FDA-approved bone-targeting radiopharmaceutical for refractory painful metastases that can also produce bone-scan-like images.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. This tests the therapeutic radiopharmaceutical that is also imageable.

fields.Text: Sacral insufficiency fracture due to osteoporosis is a classic cause of {{c1::sacral uptake on bone scintigraphy}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. The Notion entry listed this as a diagnosis prompt; no reliable diagnostic image was captured for that prompt.

fields.Text: Diffuse or concerning bone-scan findings in a 58-year-old man should raise concern for {{c1::osseous metastases}} and prompt radiographic correlation/PSA testing when appropriate.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. This preserves the testable point without forcing an uncertain visual card.

fields.Text: Four-phase bone scintigraphy adds delayed imaging at about {{c1::18-24 hours}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. The extra delayed phase helps when standard delayed images have excessive background.

fields.Text: Three-phase bone scintigraphy is useful for {{c1::osteomyelitis vs cellulitis, painful joint prosthesis, CRPS, and acute fracture/stress injury}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. These are the listed high-yield indications.

fields.Text: Four-phase bone scintigraphy is useful when a {{c1::renal failure patient needs three-phase imaging}} because background activity remains high at 2-3 hours.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. The delayed fourth phase improves skeletal-to-background contrast.

fields.Text: The flow phase of three-phase bone scintigraphy is acquired {{c1::immediately during injection}} and reflects {{c2::regional arterial perfusion}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Know timing and physiology for each phase.

fields.Text: The blood pool phase of three-phase bone scintigraphy is acquired {{c1::within minutes}} and reflects {{c2::vascular and soft tissue perfusion/hyperemia}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Know timing and physiology for each phase.

fields.Text: The delayed skeletal phase of three-phase bone scintigraphy is acquired about {{c1::2-3 hours after injection}} and reflects {{c2::tracer bound to bone}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. This is the classic delayed bone scan phase.

fields.Text: A cortical desmoid typically shows {{c1::normal activity}} on bone scintigraphy.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Not every benign bone lesion is avid.

fields.Text: A bone island typically shows {{c1::normal activity}} on bone scintigraphy.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Not every sclerotic lesion is bone-scan avid.

fields.Text: An osseous hemangioma typically shows {{c1::normal activity}} on bone scintigraphy.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Not every benign vertebral/bone lesion is avid.

fields.Text: {{c1::Osteoblastoma, osteoid osteoma, fibrous dysplasia, and giant cell tumor}} usually show intense delayed activity on bone scintigraphy.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. These benign/aggressive benign lesions can be markedly bone-scan avid.

fields.Text: Chondroblastoma can show {{c1::moderate or intense}} activity on bone scintigraphy.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. This is less uniformly intense than osteoid osteoma/osteoblastoma.

fields.Text: Enchondroma can show {{c1::mild to moderate}} activity on bone scintigraphy.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Mild uptake does not automatically imply aggressive disease.

fields.Text: Fibrous cortical defects and nonossifying fibromas typically show {{c1::normal or mild}} uptake on bone scintigraphy.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. These are usually not intensely avid.

fields.Text: An isolated rib lesion on bone scintigraphy in a patient with prostate cancer has only about a {{c1::1%}} risk of being cancer.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Isolated rib uptake is commonly benign/fracture-related.

fields.Text: The next step for an isolated rib lesion on bone scintigraphy in a patient with prostate cancer is {{c1::chest CT}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. CT correlation helps distinguish fracture from metastasis.

fields.Text: Rib lesions on bone scintigraphy are more suspicious for malignancy when they involve the {{c1::first or second ribs}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Location modifies the probability of metastasis.

fields.Text: Rib uptake is more suspicious for cancer when there is {{c1::first/second rib involvement, multiple foci in the same rib, or elongated uptake}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. These are the three listed malignant features.

fields.Text: A normal gallbladder ejection fraction is {{c1::greater than about 35%}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. The note lists about 33% for Ensure and 38% for a meal.

fields.Text: Level I cervical nodes are {{c1::anterior to the posterior aspect of the submandibular glands}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Level I includes IA and IB.

fields.Text: Level I cervical nodes are divided into {{c1::IA submental}} and {{c2::IB submandibular}} nodes.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. This is the basic Level I subdivision.

fields.Text: Level IA nodes are between the {{c1::medial margins of the anterior bellies of the digastric muscles}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. IA is the submental compartment.

fields.Text: Level IB nodes are {{c1::lateral to the anterior belly of the digastric muscle}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. IB is the submandibular compartment.

fields.Text: Level II nodes span from the {{c1::skull base}} to the {{c2::lower body of the hyoid}}, posterior to the submandibular gland and anterior to posterior SCM.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. This matches the diagram card for Level II.

fields.Text: The mandibular ramus lies between the {{c1::medial pterygoid medially}} and {{c2::masseter laterally}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. This helps localize adjacent nodal/anatomic compartments.

fields.Text: The jugular lymph node group comprises levels {{c1::II, III, and IV}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. These are the main internal jugular chain levels.

fields.Text: Level III nodes span from the {{c1::lower body of the hyoid}} to the {{c2::lower cricoid arch}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. This is the middle jugular level.

fields.Text: Levels II-IV are generally {{c1::posteromedial to SCM}} and {{c2::anterolateral to the scalene muscles}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. This is the rule-of-thumb localization.

fields.Text: Level V cervical nodes are the {{c1::posterior triangle}} nodes.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. Level V lies posterior to SCM.

fields.Text: Level V nodes are {{c1::posterior to the sternocleidomastoid muscle}}.
fields.Extra: Nukes Quiz Session. This is the key anatomic relationship.

skipped.reason: The Notion entry includes two images and a prompt, but no answer text was captured next to that prompt; no card was created rather than guessing.
